  • Changelog

Articles

Articles

Getting Started with selection.index

Quick-start guide for the selection.index R package. Learn to compute multi-trait phenotypic selection indices (LPSI) with economic weights, genotypic and phenotypic variance-covariance matrices, and rank plant breeding genotypes by predicted genetic advance.

The Linear Phenotypic Selection Index Theory

Mathematical theory and R implementation of the Linear Phenotypic Selection Index (LPSI). Covers derivation of optimal index coefficients, maximized selection response, expected genetic gain per trait, and heritability of the index using the selection.index package.

Constrained Linear Phenotypic Selection Indices

Theory and R implementation of constrained phenotypic selection indices: Null Restricted LPSI (RLPSI), Predetermined Proportional Gains LPSI (PPG-LPSI), and Desired Gains LPSI (DG-LPSI). Learn to impose zero or proportional gain restrictions on plant breeding traits.

Linear Marker and Genome-Wide Selection Indices

Marker-assisted selection in plant breeding using the Linear Marker Selection Index (LMSI) and Genome-Wide LMSI (GW-LMSI). Covers calculation of marker scores, ridge regression, and combining phenotypic and SNP marker data to maximize genetic advance in the selection.index R package.

Linear Genomic Selection Indices

Linear Genomic Selection Index (LGSI) and Combined LGSI (CLGSI) for plant breeding using genomic estimated breeding values (GEBVs). Covers mathematical theory, GEBV computation via ridge regression, cycle-length efficiency, and R implementation with the selection.index package.

Constrained Linear Genomic Selection Indices

Restricted and predetermined proportional gain linear genomic selection indices (RLGSI, PPG-LGSI, CRLGSI, CPPG-LGSI) for genomic plant breeding. Maximize genetic advance for target traits while enforcing null or proportional gain restrictions using GEBVs in the selection.index R package.

Linear Phenotypic Eigen Selection Index Methods

Eigen Selection Index Method (ESIM), Restricted ESIM (RESIM), and Predetermined Proportional Gain ESIM (PPG-ESIM) for plant breeding. Estimate optimal economic weights using canonical correlation and SVD without prior weight specification, implemented in the selection.index R package.

Linear Genomic Eigen Selection Index Methods

Genomic eigen selection index methods using GEBVs and marker data: MESIM, GESIM, GW-ESIM, RGESIM, and PPG-GESIM. Integrate genome-wide marker information into eigen-based selection frameworks to maximize genetic advance in plant breeding with the selection.index R package.

Multistage Phenotypic Selection Indices

Multistage linear selection indices (MLPSI, MRLPSI, MPPG-LPSI, MLGSI, MRLGSI, MPPG-LGSI) for sequential multi-environment plant breeding programs. Adjust phenotypic and genomic covariance matrices for prior selection with the Cochran-Cunningham method using the selection.index R package.

Stochastic Simulation of Linear Phenotypic Selection Indices

Stochastic simulation of recurrent plant breeding selection cycles using LPSI, ESIM, RLPSI, and RESIM indices. Evaluate long-term genetic gain, variance decay, and selection accuracy over 50+ generations with the selection.index R package’s simulate_selection_cycles function.
